content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 14, 2011


Ronald E. Smith
Box 2566
Ventura, CA 93002

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-11-216

Dear Mr. Smith:

     This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

     The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a), copy enclosed.) Therefore, unless you are an authorized representative of the officials you ask about, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B) and (c)(1).)

     In addition, your question involves an analysis of decisions, all of which have already occurred. Please note that the Commission will not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

     Thus, we must decline to give advice at this time. You may wish to contact our enforcement division with your concerns and file a formal complaint. For your information, we have enclosed fact sheets that describe conflicts of interest and the complaint process in detail.


___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. W-11-216
Page No. 2


If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

ER:jgl

Enclosures

sections.conclusion: Thus, we must decline to give advice at this time. You may wish to contact our enforcement division with your concerns and file a formal complaint.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought FPPC advice regarding potential conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act involving certain officials.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requester is not an authorized representative of the officials in question, and the request involves analysis of past conduct, which falls outside the Commission's advisory scope.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present, but the substance of both is evident in the body text. The document clearly explains two grounds for declination: lack of standing and retroactive nature of the inquiry. Letter dated November 14, 2011; File No. W-11-216.

QUESTION: The requester sought FPPC advice regarding potential conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act involving certain officials.

CONCLUSION: Thus, we must decline to give advice at this time. You may wish to contact our enforcement division with your concerns and file a formal complaint.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice on a conflict of interest matter because the requester lacks standing as an authorized representative and the request concerns past decisions rather than prospective guidance. The Commission referred the requester to the enforcement division for filing a formal complaint.
